era_context: The Middle Bronze Age (MBA) in the Levant and Egypt; the Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia. Egypt's 13th Dynasty was in decline; Hyksos infiltration into the Delta was ongoing. In Mesopotamia, Hammurabi had died c. 1750 BCE; his son Samsu-iluna was ruling Babylon. The Indus Valley Civilization was in its mature phase. No major civilizational shifts are documented for this specific year.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year falls in a period of sparse documentation. No specific events are attested for -1729 in surviving sources. The broader regional contexts (Samsu-iluna's reign in Babylon, 13th Dynasty decline in Egypt, Hyksos infiltration) are well-established, but no discrete historical events are recorded for this particular year.
